SUMMARY:
IoTBridge Edge Gateway offers a reliable and flexible monitoring solution. It supports both wireless sensors and wired sensors locally installed on the bridge.

FULL CONTENT:

![](https://usercontent.one/wp/www.iotbridge.se/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/cropped-iotbridge_transparantbg.png?media=1641573162)
![iot_bridge white](https://usercontent.one/wp/www.iotbridge.se/wp-content/uploads/elementor/thumbs/iot_bridge-white-p40tsk35f8t7cy9c5s9ljwsma49hjn0e28eivv44m6.png?media=1641573162 "iot_bridge white")

## Digitalisation Platform for Connected Bridges

### Cost-Effective

Our bridge monitoring platform offers cost-effective setup, monitoring and assessment of the health condition of bridges. We provide end-to-end solutions all the way from instrumentation of sensors on the bridge, continous measuring, high speed data transfer to cloud for analytics, visualisation and decision support.

### Scientific

We go beyond traditional measure and display applications. The data streams we collect are fed into algorithms to automatically assess the health status and condition of the bridge. Our algorithms are developed by world leading researchers in structural engineering, are well-proven and based on solid scientific knowledge.

### User Friendly

Our services are presented through well-designed user applications and smart phone apps for advanced visualisations providing new insights. We are using open standard data formats and the results can be directly exported into external applications such as asset management, maintenance and operations planning

![](https://usercontent.one/wp/www.iotbridge.se/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/MainHigh.png?media=1641573162)
![](https://usercontent.one/wp/www.iotbridge.se/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/iotbridgecloud-1024x554.png?media=1641573162)

### IoTBridge Cloud

IoTBridge Cloud is at the heart of our bridge monitoring system. It is a highly scalable solution able to process incoming observations from sensors mounted on a bridge such as strain gauges, accelerometers and inclinometers. It can handle frequencies up to 400 Hz. IoTBridge Cloud hosts a digital twin of the bridge and uses the latest IoT data streaming technology to translate raw data into relevant properties of the bridge. Data is processed and stored in an open standard format

### IoTBridge Analytics

Our Bridge Monitoring System offers a modern solution for analysing and assessing the structural health of a bridge. We combine state-of-the-art continuous data streaming, edge computing, advanced message brokers, cloud processing and machine learning algorithms with visualisation in smart phone apps for creating understanding and insights. The IoTBridge library of machine learning algorithms allows you to perform operations such as damage detection, rainflow analysis, frequency spectrum analysis, condition assessements and life time assessments. The apps visualises critical aspects such as stress, strain and vibrations. It allows a bridge engineer at anytime and from anywhere check the status of the bridge.

![](https://usercontent.one/wp/www.iotbridge.se/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/iotbridgeapp-1-1024x757.png?media=1641573162)
![](https://usercontent.one/wp/www.iotbridge.se/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/edgegatewayhome-1024x594.png?media=1641573162)

### IoTBridge Edge Gateway

IoTBridge Edge Gateway offers a reliable and flexible monitoring solution. It supports both wireless sensors and wired sensors locally installed on the bridge. This  reduces the instrumentation cost of the sensor system. The IoTBridge Edge Gateway is powerful and can intelligently make local computations and analysis depending on the application needs. In this way it offloads the cloud processing and increases the overall system performance. Measurements are transferred to the IoTBridge Cloud over standard mobile connections not requiring any expensive proprietary communication solutions.

Wireless Data Transmission Solution Using Wireless Bridges for Remote Operation and Maintenance in Wind Farms

In today's era of vigorous development of renewable energy, wind power generation, as the mainstay of clean energy, is transforming the energy landscape at an unprecedented pace. However, wind farms are often located in remote areas with complex environments. How to achieve efficient and stable remote operation and maintenance has become an urgent challenge for the industry. As a "veteran" with years of experience in the field of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT), this article discusses the wireless data transmission solution using wireless bridges for remote operation and maintenance in wind farms.

1. Pain Points in Wind Farm Operation and Maintenance and Opportunities for Wireless Bridges  
Wind farms are typically situated in remote regions such as mountainous areas, deserts, or offshore locations, with widely distributed wind turbines and harsh environments. Traditional operation and maintenance methods rely on wired networks, but laying optical cables is not only costly but also difficult in terms of construction and subsequent maintenance. Especially in areas with complex terrain and weak infrastructure, wired networks are even harder to implement.

The emergence of wireless bridges has brought a new solution for remote operation and maintenance in wind farms. By transmitting data via wireless signals, wireless bridges eliminate the need for laying physical cables, enabling rapid and flexible establishment of network connections with wide coverage and strong adaptability. Whether spanning valleys, rivers, deserts, or oceans, wireless bridges can easily handle the task, providing robust support for remote monitoring, fault diagnosis, and operation and maintenance management of wind farms.

2. Technical Advantages and Application Scenarios of Wireless Bridges

III. Practical Cases of Wireless Bridges in Remote Operation and Maintenance of Wind Farms  
Case 1: Remote Monitoring System in a Mountainous Wind Farm  
A mountainous wind farm faced challenges in covering the area with traditional wired networks due to complex terrain. The project team adopted a wireless bridge solution, using a point-to-multipoint networking mode to transmit real-time video data from surveillance cameras and sensors on each wind turbine to the monitoring center. After the system went live, 24/7 uninterrupted monitoring of wind turbines was achieved. Operation and maintenance personnel can view the operating status and surrounding environment of wind turbines at any time, greatly improving operation and maintenance efficiency and safety.

Case 2: Fault Diagnosis System in an Offshore Wind Farm  
An offshore wind farm faced harsh environments such as strong sea winds and salt spray corrosion, making it difficult for traditional wired networks to operate stably for a long time. The project team adopted wireless bridges with strong electromagnetic interference protection and RF environment adaptive technology, enabling real-time monitoring of key parameters such as vibration and temperature of wind turbines. Through big data analysis, the system can provide early warning of potential faults, reducing downtime and maintenance costs.

Case 3: Operation and Maintenance Management System in a Desert Wind Farm  
A desert wind farm, due to its vast area and sparse distribution of wind turbines, had inefficient traditional operation and maintenance methods. The project team adopted a solution combining wireless bridges with [4G](https://www.pusr.com/addons/cms/go/index.html?id=7)/[5G](https://www.pusr.com/addons/cms/go/index.html?id=6) networks to achieve remote monitoring and operation and maintenance management of wind turbines. Operation and maintenance personnel can view the operating status and fault information of wind turbines in real-time through mobile apps or computers, enabling remote diagnosis and maintenance guidance, greatly improving operation and maintenance efficiency and management level.

IV. Practical Experience in Wireless Bridge Selection and Deployment

V. Future Prospects of Wireless Bridges in Remote Operation and Maintenance of Wind Farms  
With the continuous development of technologies such as the Internet of Things, big data, and artificial intelligence, the application prospects of wireless bridges in remote operation and maintenance of wind farms will be even broader. In the future, wireless bridges will be deeply integrated with these technologies to achieve more intelligent and efficient operation and maintenance management.

Intelligent Operation and Maintenance: Through in-depth analysis of data transmitted by wireless bridges using artificial intelligence algorithms, automatic fault diagnosis and early warning can be achieved, improving operation and maintenance efficiency and accuracy.  
Efficient Transmission: Adopt more advanced modulation and demodulation technologies and coding algorithms to improve the transmission efficiency and bandwidth utilization of wireless bridges, meeting the increasing data transmission needs of wind farms.  
Green Operation and Maintenance: Combine with new energy technologies, such as solar power systems, to provide green and sustainable energy support for wireless bridges, reducing operation and maintenance costs and environmental impact.

As one of the important technologies in the field of IIoT, wireless bridges are playing an increasingly important role in remote operation and maintenance of wind farms. They not only solve the problems of difficult coverage and high costs associated with traditional wired networks but also provide strong support for the intelligent and efficient operation and maintenance of wind farms.

# IoT for Bridge Monitoring

![](https://www.nextind.eu/wp-content/uploads/bridge-low-next-industries.jpg "bridge-low-next-industries")

#### Abstract

**Bridge Monitoring** is a crucial application for ensuring the safety of people. Bridges are vulnerable to various factors such as human activity, water action, and soil handling, which can compromise their integrity. Developing a reliable bridge monitoring system posed a significant challenge, particularly due to the presence of multiple measurement nodes, each with its specific points of measurement. Wired systems entail the installation of numerous cables along the bridge, further complicating the process.

To address these challenges, we adopted advanced **smart sensors**, including **accelerometers**, **temperature** **sensors**, and **inclinometers**. These innovative sensors are seamlessly integrated into our wireless interface, providing a comprehensive and efficient solution. Our **wireless interface** utilizes the **[NI400](https://www.nextind.eu/smart-monitoring/iot-devices/iot-gateways/ni400/) with SigFox** radio, which boasts **low-power consumption**, allowing prolonged battery operation. This wireless interface facilitates intelligent communication and interaction among sensors, devices, and cloud portals.

By harnessing the power of **smart sensors** and **wireless interfaces**, our [bridge monitoring system](https://www.nextind.eu/smart-iot-applications/structural-health-monitoring/bridge-monitoring-systems/) empowers engineers and authorities to conduct effective [structural health monitoring](https://www.nextind.eu/smart-iot-applications/structural-health-monitoring/) of bridges. With timely and accurate data, potential safety risks can be identified and addressed promptly, ensuring the continuous safety of bridges and the people who rely on them.

#### Purpose

Next Industries is an Italian data acquisition systems developer and manufacturer whose products deals also with structural monitoring applications. The Company is young, born in 2014, but it has already had experience in geotechnical applications; so when it was requested to deal with the monitoring system of a bridge, it accepted the challenge, strong with its skilled staff and the most modern technologies now on the market.

#### Bridge Monitoring is a critical application to ensure the safety of people who cross the bridge. A bridge is, in fact, subject not only to excavations or soil handling provided by human activity but also to water action(s) around pillars.

Correct planning is the basis for successful bridge monitoring, as the primary function of data acquisition is to ensure structure longevity and safety as well as timely focused intervention in case of damage. First of all, we had to decide which devices best fitted the application, and we found some sensor types:

The strain gauge is also a useful tool, but we decided not to use it because it needs to be embedded in concrete to work properly and to monitor the curing process, so it needs to be placed during construction.

Then, we had to face the problem of **many nodes of measure** with different measure points each; **in the case of wired systems, this involves the presence of many cables running aside the bridge with the increased risk of fault possibility and noise introduction** – thus degrading the sensor signal. A single data logger solution as a concentration point is deprecated.

To propose good and accurate monitoring solutions, we had to keep clear in mind the critical parameters for the whole monitoring system (sensors and interface);

![structural bridge monitoring systems](https://www.nextind.eu/wp-content/uploads/smart-bridge-monitoring-next-industries-1024x683.jpg)

![structural bridge monitoring systems](https://www.nextind.eu/wp-content/uploads/smart-bridge-monitoring-next-industries-1024x683.jpg)

#### Next Industries is the second Italian Company with a Sigfox-ready device; thanks to the union between Next’s and Sigfox’s technologies it is possible to build up wireless networks with “intelligent” devices always on and small amounts of data sending. Exactly what we needed to complete our work successfully.

NI-400 with SigFox radio is a low-power wireless interface that allows long-term**battery operation and smart interaction among sensors, devices, and cloud portals**. Next Industries has provided for this customer **3-channels smart sensors** which were set up to send 3 acquisitions through Sigfox connection every 2 hours and which have allowed an accurate monitoring of the bridge.

A [NI400](https://www.nextind.eu/smart-monitoring/iot-devices/iot-gateways/ni400/) device was placed in every measure node, to monitor every joint, pillar, and truss; all these recorded data have been stored and shown through Next Industries’ IoT Cloud Web Portal, which also enables mail alarms and data analysis together with real-time control of field devices. One of the most appreciated features of this Portal is the possibility to create different dashboards and to customize them through widgets like Gauges, Gauges combinations, Inclinometers, Line Charts, Histograms, Tables, Maps (to geolocalize sensor/daq system), Images (for example, a picture of the bridge with sensor’s reading overlay where each sensor position is showed). Thanks to the use of NI-400 SigFox, the following results have been achieved;

#### **This solution’s key feature is the possibility of using the same monitoring and control system in a wide variety of environments, including [smart water](https://www.nextind.eu/smart-iot-applications/environmental-monitoring/smart-water-systems/), [environmental](https://www.nextind.eu/smart-iot-applications/environmental-monitoring/), exploration, and numerous other science applications.**

### Wireless Bridge Monitoring Installation Method: Injecting New Momentum into Traditional Industries with IoT Technology

With the continuous development of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) technology, traditional industries are beginning to recognize its immense potential value. Among various IoT technologies, wireless bridges, as efficient and flexible wireless transmission devices, have gradually become an indispensable part of monitoring systems. This article will introduce the installation method of [**wireless bridge**](https://www.pusr.com/products/wireless-bridge.html) monitoring in detail, helping traditional industries better understand and apply this technology.

### I. Overview of Wireless Bridge

A wireless bridge, also known as a wireless bridge connector, is a device that utilizes wireless transmission to enable communication between two or more network devices. It eliminates the need for complex wiring, simplifies network cabling, and reduces costs through wireless signals. Additionally, wireless bridges boast advantages such as long transmission distances, strong anti-interference capabilities, and high transmission rates, making them suitable for transmitting monitoring data in complex environments.

### II. Steps for Installing Wireless Bridge Monitoring

#### 1. Site Survey and Planning

Before installing a wireless bridge, a site survey is necessary to understand the location and number of monitoring points as well as the impact of the surrounding environment on wireless signals. Based on the survey results, a reasonable installation plan is formulated to determine the installation location, quantity, and transmission path of the wireless bridge.

#### 2. Equipment Selection and Procurement

Select appropriate wireless bridge equipment based on the site survey results and installation plan. During equipment selection, consider factors such as transmission distance, transmission rate, and anti-interference capability to ensure stable transmission of monitoring data. At the same time, pay attention to the compatibility and scalability of the equipment to facilitate system upgrades and expansions in the future.

#### 3. Installing Brackets and Securing Equipment

Install brackets at the selected installation locations, ensuring their stability and reliability. Then, fix the wireless bridge equipment onto the brackets, taking care to align the antenna direction with the transmission path to improve signal transmission quality. During installation, also consider waterproof, dustproof, and lightning protection measures to ensure the equipment operates normally in harsh environments.

#### 4. Connecting Network Cables and Power Supply

Connect the wireless bridge equipment to monitoring cameras, switches, and other devices using high-quality network cables. Pay attention to the length and quality of the cables to avoid signal attenuation due to excessive length or poor quality. Additionally, connect the equipment's power supply to a stable power outlet to ensure continuous and stable operation.

#### 5. Configuring Network Parameters

Configure the network parameters of the wireless bridge according to actual needs, including IP address, subnet mask, gateway, etc. During configuration, ensure compatibility with existing network devices to ensure normal data transmission.

#### 6. Testing and Optimization

After installation and configuration, test the wireless bridge to check the transmission quality and stability of the data. Make adjustments and optimizations promptly if any issues are found until optimal performance is achieved.

### III. Advantages of Wireless Bridge Monitoring

**1. Flexibility:** No complex wiring is required, allowing for flexible adjustment of installation locations and transmission paths based on actual needs.

**2. Long Transmission Distance:** Utilizing wireless transmission, it can achieve long-distance data transmission, meeting the needs of large-scale monitoring.

**3. Strong Anti-Interference Capability:** Employing advanced wireless communication technology, it can effectively resist external interference, ensuring stable data transmission.

**4. High Transmission Rate:** Supporting high-speed data transmission, it meets the transmission requirements of large data volumes such as high-definition video.

The installation method of wireless bridge monitoring provides traditional industries with an efficient and flexible monitoring solution. Through reasonable installation planning, equipment selection, professional installation techniques, and optimization measures, stable transmission and efficient utilization of monitoring data can be ensured. With the continuous development of IoT technology, wireless bridge monitoring will find wider applications in various fields, injecting new momentum into the digital transformation of traditional industries.

DESCRIPTION: The visualization showcases various structural health monitoring devices, including Displacement, Tilt, Strain, and Vibration SenSpots, connected to a remote transmission gateway and cloud storage, alongside a computer interface for data analysis.

URL: https://image.made-in-china.com/202f0j00BGJeWYHnyKcO/Wireless-IoT-Data-Transmission-Remote-Terminal-Unit-RS485-RTU-Industrial-Water-Meter-Flowmeter-Oil-Gas-Monitoring.webp
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates a wireless data transmission setup for bridge monitoring IoT, featuring sensors, transmitters, a TP302 RTU with a wireless communication module, a cloud platform, a smartphone, and a monitoring computer.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: Utilizing wireless sensors and bridges, data is transmitted seamlessly without physical cabling, enhancing flexibility and reducing installation costs in bridge monitoring systems.
Data collected from sensors is processed and stored on scalable cloud platforms, enabling efficient analytics, visualization, and real-time decision-making for structural health monitoring.
Engineers can access real-time data and analytics from anywhere, facilitating timely assessments and interventions, thereby improving overall bridge safety and maintenance efficiency.
